buyer_verbatim: We already have a corporate Visa from our bank.
ideal_phrases: so you've got the card-issuing side sorted
card from your bank is in place — what's actually broken about it?
anti_patterns: But our fees are lower than your bank's.
Your bank's controls are terrible — we have real-time alerts.
Most owners switch off bank cards within six months.

buyer_verbatim: We already use Brex — why would we switch?
ideal_phrases: Brex is great if you're US-based with USD spend
you've got the issuance side sorted with Brex — how does FX show up?
anti_patterns: Our FX is cheaper than Brex by 0.5%.
Brex doesn't actually work in the EU properly.
Most Series-B teams leave Brex within a year.

buyer_verbatim: Our accountant won't deal with another tool.
ideal_phrases: so you're protecting your accountant's bandwidth
another tool on your accountant's plate is the blocker
anti_patterns: Our Xero sync is automated — your accountant won't add work.
Most accountants love us once they see the integration.
Have you tried showing your accountant our integration page?

buyer_verbatim: I don't trust new fintechs with my money. We had a payroll thing go sideways with one of those startups last year.
ideal_phrases: a payroll thing going sideways is exactly why you're cautious
you got burned by a fintech last year — trust is the actual question
anti_patterns: We're not like those other fintechs — our uptime is 99.99%.
Actually we're regulated by a tier-1 partner bank, so it's safer.
Most owners feel that way until they read our regulator page.

buyer_verbatim: Look — your competitor's been emailing me weekly and they're cheaper.
ideal_phrases: weekly emails and a cheaper price — that's the offer on the table
cheaper-and-pushier is the comparison you're holding us against
anti_patterns: But our price-per-feature is actually better than theirs.
However, their FX rates are worse than ours.
Their cheaper price hides a 2% FX markup most owners miss.
